skill.name: pattern-detector
skill.description: Detect design patterns and anti-patterns in code, then recommend targeted refactoring steps.
skill.summary: Reviews code structure for common design patterns, anti-patterns, and practical refactoring options.
skill.author: CuriousLearner

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, sensitive database, and reconnaissance indicators, but review found these are false positives in Markdown documentation and code examples. No executable script, network access, credential handling, prompt injection attempt, or data exfiltration behavior was found in the reviewed SKILL.md file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Commands
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Markdown code fences and inline code examples in SKILL.md. They document JavaScript and report examples for a pattern detection skill and do not execute shell, Ruby, or other commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown fences, inline code spans, or static examples. There is no script file or instruction that runs these snippets as commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matched Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match ordinary design-pattern text, such as the word design and section headings. No cryptographic API, hash function, encryption algorithm, key handling, or security-sensitive use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain descriptive prose and headings about design patterns. No weak cryptographic primitive is present in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Database Example Misidentified as Sensitive File
low_findings.description: The sensitive SQLite alerts refer to a dependency injection example with a generic database variable. No SQLite file path, database dump, credential, or persisted sensitive artifact appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a short illustrative JavaScript example. It does not contain a .sqlite file reference or embedded database contents.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Repository Example Misidentified as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alert is inside a Repository pattern example that calls findById on a user repository. No host enumeration, process inspection, system inventory, or discovery command is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is a domain data access example inside documentation. It has no system-level discovery behavior or executable command context.

content.value_statement: Codebases often hide useful patterns and harmful anti-patterns that slow review and refactoring. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce structured pattern reports with practical recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Identifies common creational, structural, behavioral, and architectural design patterns.
Flags anti-patterns such as God Object, Spaghetti Code, Lava Flow, and Magic Numbers.
Assesses whether detected patterns fit the surrounding code and use case.
Produces a Markdown-style report with locations, severity, notes, and recommendations.
Suggests refactoring options such as dependency injection, repository layers, and simpler abstractions.
limitations: It provides analysis guidance, not an automated static analyzer implementation.
Accuracy depends on the code context supplied to the assistant.
It may miss framework-specific patterns without project conventions or architecture notes.
Recommendations require human review before changes are made to production code.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Refactoring Plan
use_cases.description: Find high-impact anti-patterns and convert them into a prioritized refactoring checklist.

use_cases.title: Review Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Check whether new abstractions improve the codebase or add unnecessary complexity.

use_cases.title: Learn Architecture Patterns
use_cases.description: Map real code examples to known patterns and understand why they help or hurt.

prompt_templates.title: Scan One File
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single class or module.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pattern-detector to review this file. List any design patterns, anti-patterns, and simple refactoring recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Feature Area
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of related services or components.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pattern-detector on this feature area. Identify recurring patterns, misplaced responsibilities, and the top three improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Architecture Fit
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before a larger refactor.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pattern-detector to assess whether the current architecture fits the product requirements. Compare the existing patterns with simpler alternatives.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Refactoring Roadmap
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a team backlog.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pattern-detector to produce a prioritized refactoring roadmap. Include severity, expected impact, risk, and validation steps for each recommendation.

output_examples.input: Review a payment service with several payment processor branches and repeated construction logic.
output_examples.output: The report identifies a Factory pattern candidate, notes duplicated creation logic, and recommends a small payment factory with explicit supported types.

output_examples.input: Review a large application class that manages sessions, data access, rendering, and notifications.
output_examples.output: The report flags a God Object, explains the maintainability impact, and proposes separate services for authentication, data, UI, and notifications.

output_examples.input: Review a utility folder with many global singleton classes.
output_examples.output: The report flags possible Golden Hammer usage and recommends dependency injection for classes that do not require global state.

best_practices: Provide representative files and explain the business purpose before asking for pattern analysis.
Ask for severity, evidence, and trade-offs so recommendations stay actionable.
Review suggested refactors with tests and team conventions before implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not treat every named pattern as a required improvement.
Do not request broad project conclusions from a single small file.
Do not apply refactoring recommendations without checking runtime behavior and tests.

faq.question: Does this skill modify code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides analysis and recommendations that a developer can review before editing code.

faq.question: Which patterns can it identify?
faq.answer: It covers common creational, structural, behavioral, and architectural patterns listed in the skill instructions.

faq.question: Can it find anti-patterns?
faq.answer: Yes. It looks for issues such as God Object, Spaghetti Code, Lava Flow, Golden Hammer, Cargo Cult, and Magic Numbers.

faq.question: Does it require a full repository?
faq.answer: No. It can review a file, folder, or selected snippets, but broader context improves accuracy.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed skill is documentation-only and the static security alerts were false positives.

faq.question: What should I include in a prompt?
faq.answer: Include the code, the intended behavior, known pain points, and any architecture constraints.
